title: dianthus campestris subsp. campestris
intro: Dianthus campestris subsp. campestris, also known as field pink or grass pink, is a species of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae, commonly found in mountain meadows and dry fields. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and skin problems due to its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Dianthus campestris subsp. campestris include reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the dried flowers in hot water or using them in tinctures, with the flowers also being used in teas and salves for topical application.
